```python
class Solution:
    def arrangeWords(self, text: str) -> str:
        words = text.split()
        words[0] = words[0].lower()
        words.sort(key=len)
        words[0] = words[0].capitalize()
        return ' '.join(words)

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution first splits the input text into words, then sorts them based on their lengths while maintaining the original order for words of the same length. It adjusts the case of the first word to match the required output format and joins the sorted words back into a sentence.
```
